title: Smoky Molten Algerian Tamarind Tagine
description: Smoky, molten, and nostalgic Algerian dish featuring a tamarind ball wrapped in a corn husk papillote. Perfect for a weeknight dinner or special occasion.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a pan over medium heat.
Add onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add garlic and cook for another minute.
Add chicken thighs and cook until browned, about 5 minutes per side.
In a large bowl, mix together tamarind paste, pomegranate molasses, cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Add cooked chicken to the bowl and toss to coat with the spice mixture.
Soak corn husks in water for 30 minutes.
Fill each corn husk with 1/4 cup of cooked rice, 1/4 cup of the spice mixture with chicken, and a tablespoon of olive oil.
Fold the corn husks to form a papillote and tie with kitchen twine.
Place the papillotes on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake in a preheated oven at 400°F (200°C) for 20-25 minutes, or until the corn husks are tender and the filling is hot and molten.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
